# NEXO Brain

> Open-source cognitive runtime with a shared brain for Claude Code, Codex, Claude Desktop, and other MCP clients (v7.0.1). NEXO Desktop is a separate closed-source companion app distributed at systeam.es/nexo-desktop; its source does not ship in this repo. Mentions of "NEXO Desktop" in changelog entries describe coordinated client releases that consume the Brain CLI / MCP contract.

NEXO Brain gives AI agents persistent memory using the Atkinson-Shiffrin cognitive architecture. It adds a local shared brain across supported clients, configurable terminal and automation backends, recovery-aware background jobs, startup preflight, runtime self-healing, smarter retrieval defaults, long-horizon overnight analysis, and 150+ MCP tools.

v7.0.1: hotfix over v7.0.0. `src/db/_core.py::DB_PATH` was the only caller still hardcoded to the legacy pre-F0.6 path `~/.nexo/data/nexo.db`. Every command that opened the process-wide DB connection (`nexo email list`, `nexo scripts list`, `nexo_task_open`, ...) silently returned empty results despite the real table being populated. Fix: DB_PATH is now transition-aware. NEXO_TEST_DB and NEXO_DB env overrides keep priority.

v7.0.0: BREAKING â€” Plan Consolidado fase F0.6. Physical separation of the runtime tree into `~/.nexo/{core,personal,runtime}/`. The flat layout (`~/.nexo/scripts/`, `brain/`, `data/`, `operations/`, ...) is gone. Operators on v6.x are auto-migrated on first `nexo update` to v7.0.0; fresh installs land directly in the new tree. New `paths.py` module centralises every runtime path helper. 24 src files refactored, 7 shell scripts updated, 71 `personal_scripts.path` rows UPDATEd transactionally, 40 LaunchAgent plists rewritten. 1551 tests pass. The companion NEXO Desktop release (v0.21.0) updates its hardcoded paths so auto-update keeps working.

v6.5.0: Plan Consolidado fase F0.2. New CLI verbs `nexo scripts enable|disable|status <name>` (all accept `--json`). The cron wrapper (`nexo-cron-wrapper.sh`) reads the `personal_scripts.enabled` flag on every tick and short-circuits to `exit 0` with `summary='[disabled]'` when the operator has the script off â€” the LaunchAgent stays loaded so re-enabling is a single CLI call. The upsert path on `personal_scripts` no longer overwrites `enabled` on the UPDATE branch, so the operator-set flag is now sticky across `nexo scripts sync` runs. New helper functions `set_personal_script_enabled` + `get_personal_script_status`. 4 new tests in `tests/test_personal_scripts_enabled.py`. The companion NEXO Desktop client (closed-source, distributed separately) wires the same toggle into its Settings â†’ Automatizaciones panel.

v6.4.0: Plan Consolidado fase F1. New `email_accounts` table (m46) stores multi-tenant mailbox config with passwords pointed at the existing `credentials` table (never duplicated). New CLI `nexo email setup` (interactive wizard for first-time operators) + `nexo email add --label X --email X --imap-host X --password-stdin --json` (non-interactive, used by NEXO Desktop v0.19.0 Email panel â€” passwords pipe through stdin so they never appear on argv). `list`/`test`/`remove` accept `--json` for machine consumers. `email_config.load_email_config()` is the single loader (DB first, legacy `~/.nexo/nexo-email/config.json` fallback). Auto-migrator runs on next session (`auto_update.py`) â€” existing operators upgrade transparently. `nexo-email-monitor.py` and `nexo-send-reply.py` now go through the new loader. 8 tests in `tests/test_email_accounts.py`. Bandit-clean (`_debt_fingerprint` SHA1 marked `usedforsecurity=False`).

v6.3.1: privacy hotfix â€” remove operator-specific vhost_mapping entries that v6.3.0 accidentally shipped inside the public `entities_universal.json`. Operator private entries now live in `~/.nexo/brain/presets/entities_local.json` (`.gitignore`'d, seeded from `entities_local.sample.json`).

v6.3.0: Plan Consolidado wave 2, coordinated with NEXO Desktop v0.18.0. Extended `cognitive_sentiment` shape (`is_correction`, `valence`, `intent` enum); entities schema gains `aliases`/`metadata`/`source`/`confidence`/`access_mode` via idempotent migration m44; 21 labelled rule fixtures + R13 spike gates (FP < 5%, P95 < 3s); `artifact_class` preset entries (`shopify_banner_block`, `changelog_entry`, `email_to_operator_contact`); R34 identity-coherence added to the system prompt with an explicit anti-example; Fase F telemetry loops (`fase_f_loops` + Deep Sleep `phase_guardian_analysis` report); pinned local zero-shot classifier skeleton (`MoritzLaurer/mDeBERTa-v3-base-mnli-xnli` at revision `a1a5a76a8cb44edb4f92e7e2ea4f0e0ce8ce6e97`) plus `docs/classifier-model-notes.md`; `process_pre_tool_event` respects `NEXO_MIGRATING=1`; `origin` column on `personal_scripts` via m45; T4 LLM gate wraps R15/R23e/R23f/R23h with byte-for-byte Py â†” JS parity. `enforcement_classifier.classify` now supports `tristate=True` so an unparseable conservative fallback returns `"unknown"` instead of silently suppressing destructive rules.

v6.1.1: small fix â€” `nexo --help` refreshes `Latest: vX` via npm even when invoked via subprocess with piped stdio. Prior TTY gate in `_should_refresh_latest_version()` blocked NEXO Desktop's subprocess from ever populating the version cache, so the Brain auto-update banner never saw a newer version and users had to run `nexo update` manually. Fix removes the TTY gate; 6-hour `_load_latest_version_cache` stays as the real rate-limit; fail-closes to installed-only when npm unreachable.

v6.1.0: Protocol Enforcer Fase 2 ships. Capa 2 runtime guardian with 25 rules (R13 pre-Edit guard, R14 post-correction window, R16 declared-done, R25 Nora/MarĂ­a read-only block, plus 21 more across stream wrapper layers C/D/D2). `src/enforcement_engine.py` (Python headless) + `enforcement-engine.js` (NEXO Desktop) byte-for-byte template parity. Companion migration v43 `session_claude_aliases` fixes NEXO Desktop multi-conversation edits blocked with "unknown target": each Claude spawn's SessionStart hook now registers an alias so `_resolve_nexo_sid` resolves every conversation's UUID to the same NEXO sid. External-LLM audit + Opus 4.7 self-audit cycle applied: log redaction covers Bearer/sk-/pk-/api_key/JWT/AWS/GitHub/Shopify/KEY=VALUE/mysql -p<pass>, R23f heredoc multiline, R23h native PATH resolution (no shell injection), R14 detector awaited, cross-engine parity harness strict mode. Red-team suite (32 attempts across 12 rules). Guardian telemetry NDJSON + `scripts/install_guardian.py` installer + `docs/guardian-quickstart.md` operator guide. 291 pass + 2 skip (documented: `-F` commit-msg is not `--force`; `--force-with-lease` is safe by spec).

v6.0.6: installer no longer leaks `export PATH="$NEXO_HOME/bin:$PATH"` into the developer's real shell profile when `NEXO_HOME` is non-canonical. `src/auto_update.py::_ensure_runtime_cli_in_shell()` and both `bin/nexo-brain.js` call sites (install Step 8 + migration path) now consult `_should_skip_shell_profile_backfill()` / `shouldSkipShellProfileBackfill()`. Fresh installs at `$HOME/.nexo` are unaffected; operators can force-skip via `NEXO_SKIP_SHELL_PROFILE=1`. Root cause: the rc-file list was computed from `Path.home()` / `os.homedir()` regardless of `NEXO_HOME`, so any pytest run with `NEXO_HOME=/tmp/pytest-xxx` polluted `~/.bash_profile` / `~/.bashrc` / `~/.zshrc` on the developer's real machine. Five new regression tests in `tests/test_auto_update_shell_profile.py`. CI gate `tests.yml` introduced in v6.0.5 catches this category of drift. Also cleans a duplicate `.github/workflows/tests 2.yml` committed by accident. 1098 passed, 2 xfailed, 1 skipped.
v6.0.4: interactive launchers now honour `preferences.default_resonance`. Pre-v6.0.4 both `nexo chat` (`build_interactive_client_command`) and the dashboard's "Open followup in Terminal" action (`build_followup_terminal_shell_command`) picked `--model` / `--effort` straight from `config/schedule.json`'s `client_runtime_profiles`, so users who switched their Resonance in NEXO Desktop Preferences (Alto â†’ writes `brain/calibration.json`) kept getting the stale tier cached in the legacy profile (usually `max`). Headless runs (`run_automation_prompt`) and NEXO Desktop new-sessions already honoured the preference correctly; only the two terminal launchers were stuck. New `_resolve_interactive_model_and_effort(caller, backend, ...)` helper consults `resonance_map.resolve_model_and_effort` first and falls back to `client_runtime_profiles` only when the resonance contract is missing. `run_automation_interactive` propagates `caller=` and `tier=` into the builder; `nexo_followup_terminal` joins `nexo_chat` / `desktop_new_session` / `nexo_update_interactive` in `USER_FACING_CALLERS` so the dashboard launcher resolves against the user's preference. 8 tests updated, full suite green.
v6.0.3: double-fix release. (1) `resonance_tiers.json` now lives at the public contract path `~/.nexo/brain/resonance_tiers.json` on install and update; pre-v6.0.3 it was written to `~/.nexo/` and NEXO Desktop â‰Ą 0.12.0 failed to start Claude with *"NEXO Brain contract missing"* on every fresh install and every update from 6.0.0 / 6.0.1 / 6.0.2. New `publishBrainContracts()` helper in `bin/nexo-brain.js`, three-step lookup in `src/resonance_map.py` (`NEXO_HOME/brain/` â†’ legacy `NEXO_HOME/` â†’ `src/`), and an idempotent `_relocate_resonance_tiers_contract(dest)` migration in `auto_update.py` that promotes legacy runtimes on the next `nexo update` and unlinks the old copy. (2) `nexo_guard_check` now writes the caller's SID into `guard_checks.session_id` instead of the empty string that slipped in in v6.0.2. Resolution walks `NEXO_SID` env â†’ `CLAUDE_SESSION_ID` env translated via `sessions.external_session_id` â†’ most-recently-updated `sessions` row. Strict-protocol sessions stop tripping *"no guard_check seen for this session"* right after a successful call. 9 new pytest cases, full suite stays green.
v6.0.2: reserved caller prefix `personal/` for user-owned scripts outside the NEXO Brain repo. Any script living in `~/.nexo/scripts/` can now invoke the automation backend with its own caller id (`caller="personal/<descriptive-id>"`) and pick a resonance tier (`tier="maximo"|"alto"|"medio"|"bajo"`) without editing `src/resonance_map.py`. Precedence for personal callers: explicit `tier=` â†’ explicit `reasoning_effort=` â†’ `calibration.preferences.default_resonance` â†’ `DEFAULT_RESONANCE` ("alto"). Invalid tier values fall through silently, never crash. New kwarg `tier` on `run_automation_prompt`, `run_automation_interactive`, `nexo_helper.run_automation_text`, `nexo_helper.run_automation_json`; `--tier` / `--caller` flags on `nexo-agent-run.py`. New guide `docs/personal-scripts-guide.md` for any NEXO session helping an operator author a personal script. Registered callers (`USER_FACING_CALLERS` / `SYSTEM_OWNED_CALLERS`) keep v6.0.0 behaviour; non-`personal/` callers without a registry entry still raise `UnregisteredCallerError`. 3 new pytest modules (14 cases), suite stays green (1079 passed).
v6.0.1: hotfix on top of 6.0.0. `protocol_settings.py` now treats the process as interactive when either stdin+stdout are TTYs or `NEXO_INTERACTIVE=1` is exported â€” NEXO Desktop 0.12.0 spawns `claude` through pipes and fell back to `lenient` in v6.0.0. Truthy-looking values (`true`/`yes`/`on`) are rejected; only the literal `"1"` opts in. The `PostToolUse` hook gains an inbox-autodetect stage that emits a `systemMessage` when the session has unread `nexo_send` messages and 60s+ have passed since the last heartbeat; rate-limited to one reminder per minute per SID via the new `hook_inbox_reminders` table (migration m42). `sessions.last_heartbeat_ts` is stamped by `nexo_heartbeat` on every invocation and read by the hook to decide whether to surface the reminder. `NEXO_INTERACTIVE` is an internal Brainâ†”Electron contract; it is not user-facing and is not a resurrection of the removed `NEXO_PROTOCOL_STRICTNESS`. 6 new pytest modules, full suite stays green (1065 passed).
v6.0.0: BREAKING. Tier-only setup. Onboarding asks one resonance tier (`maximo`/`alto`/`medio`/`bajo`) and `src/resonance_tiers.json` becomes the single source of truth for the per-backend `(model, effort)` map consumed by `src/resonance_map.py` via `load_resonance_table()`. Legacy `client_runtime_profiles.{claude_code,codex}.{model,reasoning_effort}` purged from `schedule.json` on upgrade. Protocol strictness is no longer configurable â€” TTY â†’ `strict`, no-TTY â†’ `lenient`; `NEXO_PROTOCOL_STRICTNESS` env, `preferences.protocol_strictness`, and the `default/normal/off/warn/soft` aliases removed. `preferences.show_pending_at_start` moves to NEXO Desktop electron-store. The seven core hooks unified behind `src/hooks/manifest.json` (plugin and npm modes read the same file); two new hooks ship: `Notification` â†’ `hook_observability.record_activity()` and `SubagentStop` â†’ auto-closes stale `protocol_tasks`. `auto_capture.py` wired to `UserPromptSubmit` + `PostToolUse` with persistent 1h dedup (`auto_capture_dedup` table) and automatic `nexo_learning_add` on correction matches. `~/.nexo/hooks_status.json` published after every `registerAllCoreHooks()` for NEXO Desktop â‰Ą0.12.0. New `nexo-brain --skip` flag aliases `--yes`/`--defaults`. `calibration_migration.apply_v6_purge()` invoked once from `auto_update._run_runtime_post_sync()`: never raises, never overwrites an existing `preferences.default_resonance`. Tests: 7 new pytests, 1057 passed, 1 skipped.
v5.10.2: auto-bootstraps `brain/profile.json` from `brain/calibration.json` on `nexo update`. Operators whose onboarding flow was interrupted before v5.9.x had `role` and `technical_level` recorded under `calibration.meta.*` only, with no `profile.json` file â€” NEXO Desktop's Preferencias â†’ Avanzado tab then rendered an empty `{}` for the profile block with no context. New `_bootstrap_profile_from_calibration_meta(dest)` runs inside `_run_runtime_post_sync()` and seeds `profile.json` with `role` / `technical_level` / `name` / `language` when any are present. Never overwrites a populated profile, never raises, idempotent. Paired with Desktop v0.11.2 which adds explanatory headers to both JSON blocks in the Avanzado tab and renders a friendly placeholder when `profile.json` is still missing. Also fixes `test_user_facing_caller_with_no_user_default_uses_alto` which was leaking the real ~/.nexo state after the v5.10.1 migration. 10 new tests (`test_auto_update_bootstrap_profile.py`). Full suite: 1021 passed, 1 skipped.
v5.10.1: silent, one-shot migration that recovers legacy `reasoning_effort="max"` (and siblings `xhigh`/`high`/`medium`) from `config/schedule.json` into `preferences.default_resonance` in `brain/calibration.json`. Any operator who configured `max` before v5.9.0 and never touched the new selector was silently falling back to `DEFAULT_RESONANCE="alto"` on interactive calls since the v5.10.0 update. New `_migrate_effort_to_resonance(dest)` runs inside `_run_runtime_post_sync()` exactly once: `maxâ†’maximo`, `xhighâ†’alto`, `highâ†’medio`, `mediumâ†’bajo`. No-op when calibration/schedule already declares `default_resonance`; idempotent; conservative; never raises. 10 new tests plus a test-harness fix that unblocks `test_cron_recovery.py` against main. Full suite: 1011 passed, 1 skipped.
v5.10.0: extract-path bloat fix + `caller=` enforced + personal scripts on the map. New `bare_mode` kwarg on `run_automation_prompt` wires `claude --bare` for JSON-only callers (skips CLAUDE.md auto-load, hooks, LSP, plugin sync, keychain probe â€” ~4.3Ă— faster per child, ~9s â†’ ~2s). `deep-sleep/extract` and `deep-sleep/synthesize` auto-enable bare when an `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is resolvable (env, `~/.claude/anthropic-api-key.txt`, `~/.nexo/config/anthropic-api-key.txt`); silent fallback to the normal path when no key is available. `caller=` is now **mandatory** â€” `run_automation_prompt` raises `UnregisteredCallerError` without a registered caller. Five personal scripts joined the map (`personal/email-monitor`, `personal/github-monitor`, `personal/post-x`, `personal/followup-runner`, `personal/orchestrator-v2`). gbp/* marketing callers bumped medio â†’ alto (public-facing copy). 65 legacy protocol debts bulk-resolved. 10 new bare-mode tests (974 total in suite).
v5.9.1: adds `default_resonance` to the Desktop-facing schema exported by `nexo schema --json`. NEXO Desktop's existing Preferences â†’ Preferences tab renders the selector automatically via its `buildFieldsFromBrainSchema()` path â€” no Desktop release required. Four localised options (`MĂˇximo` / `Alto (recomendado)` / `Medio` / `Bajo` in Spanish, `Maximum` / `High (recommended)` / `Medium` / `Low` in English). New `resonance_map._load_user_default_resonance()` reads `brain/calibration.json` first (new canonical location, where Desktop writes) and falls back to `config/schedule.json` (where the v5.9.0 CLI wrote). `nexo preferences --resonance` now writes to both. `resolve_tier_for_caller` consults the helper when `user_default` is not passed, so `nexo chat` / `launch_interactive_client` pick up the Desktop-edited value with no extra wiring. 6 new tests (26 total in resonance_map suite).
v5.9.0: centralised resonance map + unified automation session log. New `src/resonance_map.py` defines four tiers (`MAXIMO` / `ALTO` / `MEDIO` / `BAJO`) that each resolve to a concrete `(model, reasoning_effort)` pair per backend. Every caller registers in one of two dicts: `USER_FACING_CALLERS` (nexo chat, Desktop new conversation, interactive nexo update â€” honour the user's `default_resonance`) or `SYSTEM_OWNED_CALLERS` (every cron and script â€” locked at a fixed tier per caller). `deep-sleep/synthesize`, `evolution/run`, and `reflection` run `MAXIMO`; `deep-sleep/extract`, `catchup/morning`, `followup_runner` run `ALTO`; marketing copy (`gbp/*`) runs `MEDIO`. Unknown callers raise `UnregisteredCallerError`. Migration #41 adds `caller`, `session_type`, `started_at`, `ended_at`, `pid`, `resonance_tier` to `automation_runs`; interactive sessions record a row at spawn with `ended_at=NULL` and update it on close, so every Claude/Codex invocation on the machine flows through one table. New `run_automation_interactive()` covers `nexo chat` (which `launch_interactive_client` now routes through). New MCP tools `nexo_session_log_create` / `nexo_session_log_close` give NEXO Desktop the same surface for its direct-claude spawns. New `nexo preferences --resonance` CLI writes `default_resonance` into `schedule.json`. 20 new tests (resonance_map: 14, automation_sessions_log: 6); full suite 942 green.
v5.8.2: Brain core neutralised â€” removed the Spanish-first NEXO-specific classification heuristic from `src/db/_classification.py`. v5.8.0 shipped `classify_task()` / `is_internal_id()` / `classify_owner()` as fallbacks when agents omitted `internal` / `owner` on `nexo_followup_create` and `nexo_reminder_create`; they matched `NF-PROTOCOL-*` ID prefixes, Spanish user-verbs (`debes`, `revisar`, `firmar`), and agent keywords (`monitor`, `auditorĂ­a diaria`, `checkpoint`). Fine for NEXO's own DB, poison for third-party agents plugged into the shared Brain. Core now persists `internal=0` and `owner=NULL` on omission; clients that want automatic classification (NEXO Desktop does, via `_legacyClassifyOwner`/`_legacyIsInternalTaskId`) compute it themselves and pass the result. Migration #40 keeps the columns + indexes; previously backfilled rows keep their values. `normalise_owner` still explicitly rejects the string `"nexo"` so legacy hardcoding cannot sneak back in. 12 tests rewritten around the neutral contract.
v5.8.1: closes a self-reinforcing `launchctl kickstart -k` loop in the watchdog that wedged deep-sleep Phase 2 between 2026-04-14 and 2026-04-17. The cron wrapper now INSERTs an in-flight row (`ended_at=NULL`) at start and traps SIGTERM/INT/HUP to close it with `exit_code=143` plus `Killed by SIGTERM`. The watchdog interprets in-flight rows as "currently running" and only re-executes after verifying the worker process is dead. `extract.py` classifies CLI failures into transient (`overloaded_error`, rate-limit, timeout, signal â€” retried next run) vs deterministic (skipped after `MAX_POISON_ATTEMPTS`), and passes a slim shared-context (200 head lines + metadata) instead of the full 400+ KB dump. New `auto_update._heal_deep_sleep_runtime()` repairs existing installs silently on the next `nexo update`: poisoned checkpoints, stale locks, dangling `cron_runs` rows, and bloated `.watchdog-fails` counters. 22 new tests (`test_cron_wrapper_contract.py`, `test_deep_sleep_extract.py`, `test_auto_update_heal_deep_sleep.py`, `test_watchdog_in_flight.py`).
v5.8.0: first-class `internal` and `owner` columns on `followups` and `reminders`. Migration #40 adds both fields with an idempotent one-shot backfill so the "who does this task belong to?" classification moves from client-side regex (NEXO Desktop) into persistent storage every MCP client shares. Taxonomy is `owner in {user, waiting, agent, shared}` â€” `agent` is deliberately generic so non-NEXO deployments render their own assistant label instead of inheriting NEXO branding. `nexo_reminder_create`, `nexo_reminder_update`, `nexo_followup_create`, and `nexo_followup_update` gain optional `internal` and `owner` parameters. A new src/db/_classification.py centralises the heuristic (user verbs / waiting keywords / agent-owned recurrences / NF-PROTOCOL-*/NF-DS-* internal prefixes) so backfilled rows render identically to pre-migration Desktop. Closes a UX paradox where tasks labelled "Para ti" could be hidden by the "Tareas internas" filter.
v5.7.0: `nexo update` auto-updates Claude Code and Codex CLIs. New `_update_external_clis()` detects the global version of `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` and `@openai/codex`, queries the npm registry, and runs `npm install -g <pkg>@latest` when a bump is available. Packages that were never installed globally are skipped silently. Timeouts and missing npm are handled explicitly. New `--no-clis` flag on `nexo update` (and `include_clis=False` on the MCP `nexo_update` tool) pins the terminal CLIs when operators prefer manual control. Motivation: close the gap where `~/.claude/settings.json` already pointed at Opus 4.7 but the locally-installed Claude Code was too old to recognise the new model id.
v5.6.1: update-path hardening. Purge 0-byte .db orphans (interrupted installs) from ~/.nexo/ and ~/.nexo/data/ before the pre-update backup â€” they were masking the real DB and breaking validation. New sync_claude_code_model() helper propagates the NEXO-recommended model into ~/.claude/settings.json (which Claude Code actually reads) when heal_runtime_profiles() migrates the claude_code default. No-op when settings.json is missing or has no top-level "model" field.
v5.6.0: default model upgrade Opus 4.6 â†’ 4.7 with reasoning_effort "max". Auto-migration on `nexo update` silently upgrades users from claude-opus-4-6* to claude-opus-4-7, preserving 1M suffix. Codex untouched.
v5.5.6: rate-limit the backup/restore/export tools in the MCP boundary. `nexo_backup_now` capped at 1/30s, `nexo_backup_restore` at 1/60s, `export_user_bundle` at 1/120s. A runaway MCP client (tool-use loop) can no longer hammer sqlite3.Connection.backup() the way the 2026-04-16 incident did. Same-day follow-up to v5.5.5.
v5.5.5: data-loss guardrails + automatic self-heal. The updater now refuses to capture an already-wiped nexo.db into a pre-update snapshot (validated sqlite3.backup + pre-flight wipe guard + post-migration row-count gate), and an auto-heal restores ~/.nexo/data/nexo.db from the newest hourly backup on the next server boot when a wipe is detected. New `nexo recover` CLI + `nexo_recover` MCP tool with mandatory kill-MCP, pre-recover snapshot, and post-restore row-count validation.
v5.5.4: Deep Sleep no longer blocks on unparseable sessions â€” retries reduced from 3 to 2, added a JSON escape hatch so the model always returns parseable output, and unified the automation subprocess timeout to 3h (10800s) across 10 scripts via a new shared constant AUTOMATION_SUBPROCESS_TIMEOUT in src/constants.py.
v5.5.3: CLAUDE.md CORE now explains the Protocol Enforcer â€” aligned models (Opus 4.6, safety-tuned variants) stop rejecting heartbeat, diary, and checkpoint injections as suspected prompt injection. Paired with NEXO Desktop v0.9.25 which wraps enforcer prompts in <system-reminder> tags.
v5.5.2: auto-repair unloaded LaunchAgents + headless model fallback cleanup â€” verifies launchctl loaded state, migrated to modern bootstrap/bootout API, and removes legacy opus/sonnet fallbacks from core automation scripts
v5.5.1: real-time headless Protocol Enforcer â€” enforcement_engine.py monitors tool calls and injects prompts in all headless sessions via stream-json Popen
v5.4.9: headless Protocol Enforcer â€” all crons/scripts get enforcement rules via append_system_prompt automatically
v5.4.8: tool-enforcement-map v2.0 â€” multi-dimensional enforcement with must/should/may levels, dependency chains, internal calls tracking. 11 must + 10 should + 225 none
v5.4.7: tool-enforcement-map.json â€” canonical map of all 247 tools with enforcement metadata for Protocol Enforcer. Includes verify_tool_map.py for drift detection
v5.4.6: runtime dependency management â€” `nexo update` now manages external dependencies declared in `runtimeDependencies` (starting with Claude Code) + daily auto-update cron at 02:00
v5.4.5: CI timeout increase â€” subprocess timeout 10â†’30s for nexo update test on slow CI runners
v5.4.4: test isolation + venv timeout fix â€” tree_hygiene.py copy + fake venv to prevent CI timeout, completing the publish workflow fix
v5.4.3: test isolation fix â€” copy tree_hygiene.py into isolated runtime dirs so the publish workflow passes
v5.4.2: traceability truth + Sensory Register buffer close-loop â€” diary warnings now distinguish repo `commit_ref` debt from local/server-side operational changes, the nocturnal postmortem drains pending `session_buffer.jsonl` events instead of looking only at "today", rewrites the buffer atomically, and the docs now say honestly that `nexo-reflection.py` is a standalone analyzer rather than something auto-triggered by the stop hook
v5.4.1: hook hygiene fix â€” the PostToolUse `capture-session.sh` hook had been reading a nonexistent env var since 2026-04-12, silently writing `"tool":"unknown"` to `~/.nexo/brain/session_buffer.jsonl` for 48 hours. v5.4.1 parses `tool_name` from stdin JSON, removes the filter that was hiding `Bash`, deletes the contaminating `capture-session 2.sh` duplicate, and purges pre-fix entries from the buffer on update with a `.pre-v5.4.1.bak` backup
v5.4.0: runtime events + health + logs + calibration migration â€” append-only event bus at `~/.nexo/runtime/events.ndjson` with stable envelope, `nexo notify` for one-shot proactive events, `nexo health --json` for a rolled-up subsystem snapshot, `nexo logs --tail --json` for structured log access, and a safe flatâ†’nested migration for `calibration.json` that runs silently inside `nexo update` with a pre-migrate backup
v5.3.30: desktop bridge â€” four read-only CLI commands (`nexo schema`, `nexo identity`, `nexo onboard`, `nexo scan-profile`) let external UIs like NEXO Desktop render preferences, onboarding, identity, and profile heuristics from live JSON instead of hardcoding field lists and releasing in lockstep
v5.3.29: runtime hygiene + fail-closed startup + honest cron tracing â€” duplicate `* 2` artifacts now fail release gates instead of hiding in the tree, update paths converge on one canonical core, startup preflight runs synchronously, corrupt DB state no longer respawns an empty brain by default, and cron runs spool locally when SQLite is unavailable
v5.3.11: protocol + cortex contract hardening â€” malformed `outcome`, `task_type`, and `impact_level` values now fail explicitly instead of being silently coerced into other valid states, so persisted task/debt/context/decision data stays trustworthy
v5.3.10: packaged runtime truth + evolution telemetry + synthesis loop closure â€” installs and updates now keep `~/.nexo/package.json` aligned with the published npm package, deep doctor stays honest during fresh bootstrap, weekly Evolution asks for explicit dimension telemetry again, and daily synthesis only absorbs actionable startup/update summaries
v5.3.9: packaged core-artifact manifest heal â€” packaged updates now rebuild the core runtime manifest from the canonical npm package source so personal scripts stop being reclassified as core, portable exports keep including them, and runtime doctor can recover personal LaunchAgents cleanly after a bad `5.3.8` update
v5.3.8: packaged migration hotfix â€” the packaged installer now copies new top-level runtime Python modules into `~/.nexo` automatically, so newly shipped commands like `nexo export` / `nexo import` work immediately after update on real packaged installs
v5.3.7: packaged update self-heal + portable user-data bundles â€” `nexo update` now refreshes packaged crons and reloads LaunchAgents after version bumps, new `nexo export` / `nexo import` commands move operator data safely, and runtime doctor no longer treats tracked historical Codex drift as a broken install
v5.3.6: Claude MCP bootstrap + runtime hygiene hardening â€” managed Claude Code sync now writes the NEXO MCP server to the user config Claude actually reads, schedule status treats open runs honestly, and retroactive learnings stop keyword-only false positives outside declared scope
v5.3.5: version banner cache correction â€” `nexo` / `nexo chat` no longer show a stale older `Latest` value right after `nexo update`
v5.3.4: runtime boundary cleanup + version visibility â€” legacy hook aliases stay out of the personal bucket, `nexo update` removes retired aliases, and `nexo` / `nexo chat` show latest vs installed version
v5.3.3: doctor inventory alignment â€” `nexo doctor` now recognizes the built-in `com.nexo.backup` LaunchAgent as a core auxiliary service on packaged installs
v5.3.2: runtime boundary hardening â€” packaged installs track core runtime artifacts, `nexo scripts` stops mixing core with personal, and `nexo update` migrates legacy heartbeat wrappers into managed core hooks
v5.3.1: packaged runtime normalization â€” `nexo update` keeps normal npm installs anchored to `~/.nexo` and refreshes packaged artifacts cleanly
v5.3.0: nexo uninstall â€” stops crons, removes runtime, preserves user data for clean reinstall
v5.2.1: fix deep-sleep datetime crash in apply_findings.py; cortex_decide() auto-creates outcomes closing decisionâ†’verification loop
v5.2.0: bilingual response-discipline scoring + cortex quality cache reader

## Core Product Model

- Shared brain is the baseline.
- Interactive client and automation backend are configurable separately.
- Claude Code remains the recommended path because it has the deepest hook and headless automation integration.
- Codex is supported as both terminal client and automation backend.
- Claude Desktop can share the same brain through MCP.

## Key Features

- Three-store memory: Sensory Register, Short-Term Memory, Long-Term Memory
- Semantic search with local embeddings and optional HNSW acceleration
- Claim graph, trust scoring, metacognitive guard, cognitive dissonance detection
- Episodic memory, reminders, learnings, followups, and session continuity
- Context continuity across Claude Code compaction via hooks
- Shared-brain client sync for Claude Code, Codex, and Claude Desktop
- Managed bootstrap parity for `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` and `~/.codex/AGENTS.md`
- Managed Codex global bootstrap/model sync via `~/.codex/config.toml`
- Managed Codex `mcp_servers.nexo` sync inside `~/.codex/config.toml`
- Existing-install backfill so older Codex-linked runtimes actually receive that managed global bootstrap/model sync during `nexo update`
- Installed-runtime bootstrap hotfix so existing users receive those managed bootstrap updates correctly
- Bootstrap operator-name fallback so managed `CLAUDE.md` / `AGENTS.md` never render with blank identity headings
- Explicit `CORE` / `USER` contract so updates can refresh product rules without overwriting operator instructions
- Configurable `nexo chat` terminal client
- Configurable automation backend with client-specific model profiles
- Automatic HyDE / spreading activation defaults that stay conservative for exact lookups
- Retrieval explanations with confidence + auto-strategy hints
- Per-memory `stability` / `difficulty` personalization on top of Ebbinghaus decay
- Deep Sleep transcript parity across Claude Code and Codex session stores
- 60-day long-horizon Deep Sleep blend for recurring themes and older/current cross-domain connections
- Weighted project-priority signals plus weekly/monthly Deep Sleep summary artifacts
- Periodic Deep Sleep summaries with protocol compliance, engineering-loop output, project pulse, and trend-vs-previous data
- Semantic Deep Sleep followup deduplication plus learning consolidation outcomes: duplicate, reinforcement, contradiction review, or genuinely new
- Deep Sleep engineering followup backfill so recurring patterns turn into concrete scripts, hooks, checklists, and guardrails instead of only passive observations
- Runtime doctor parity audits for recent Codex sessions and Claude Desktop shared-brain metadata
- Runtime doctor audits weekly protocol compliance plus release artifact sync drift
- Dashboard narratives for what matters now, what is drifting, and what is improving
- Public `scripts/verify_release_readiness.py` validator for release discipline
- Pending Codex launcher fixes included: client selection, launch mode, last terminal choice, interactive flags
- Runtime CLI with `nexo chat`, `nexo update`, `nexo doctor`, and personal script management
- Self-healing runtime with managed schedule reconciliation and KeepAlive daemon support
- Personal scripts registry, dashboard, overnight learning, and recovery-aware jobs
- Claude Code plugin packaging, OpenClaw package, and ClawHub skill

## Recommended Defaults

- Claude Code: recommended primary client
- Claude Code model profile: Opus 4.7 with 1M context
- Codex model profile: GPT-5.4 xhigh

## Installation

```bash
npx nexo-brain
```

## Update Existing Installations

```bash
nexo update
```

## Re-sync Clients Later

```bash
nexo clients sync
```

## Main Commands

- `nexo chat` â€” open the configured terminal client
- `nexo update` â€” sync the installed runtime
- `nexo doctor` â€” audit and repair runtime health
- `nexo scripts list` â€” inspect personal scripts

## Documentation

- Website: https://nexo-brain.com
- Changelog: https://nexo-brain.com/changelog/
- Blog: https://nexo-brain.com/blog/
- Docs: https://nexo-brain.com/docs/
- GitHub: https://github.com/wazionapps/nexo
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/nexo-brain

## Compatibility

NEXO Brain exposes its capabilities as MCP tools. It is compatible with MCP-capable environments and currently has explicit shared-brain flows for Claude Code, Codex, and Claude Desktop. OpenClaw is also supported through the published package.

## Contributing

Issues and PRs are welcome, especially for client parity, installer UX, public docs, compatibility validation, and real-world runtime reliability.

AGPL-3.0. Built by WAzion.
